missing_text:
Planning to Provide a Year's Supply

Elder Mark B. Garff, Member, General Welfare Committee, and Gertrude R. Garff, former Counselor, Relief Society General Presidency

But if any provide not for his own, and specially for those of his own house, he hath denied the faith, and is worse than an infidel (I Timothy, 5:8).

THE ultimate temporal success and security of the members of the Church will be determined by the diligence of the people to make themselves self sustaining in all respects. We must understand that we are not only to sustain ourselves, but also to build up the Church financially in order that the Church as an organization may help those members who have met with misfortune and find it impossible to help themselves. Everyone is a potential recipient of help within the Welfare Plan of the Church. No one with few exceptions can justify himself in expecting the Church to take care of his need continually. The Welfare Plan was given to rehabilitate and encourage members, to bring them back to a point of self-sufficiency. The plan is not a dole. It is not a process of continually contributing to the individual, who can (when properly directed) be self-sustaining.

article_title: Planning to Provide a Year's Supply
article_author: Elder Mark B. Garff and Gertrude R. Garff
notes: Found the beginning of the article about providing a year's supply that was cut off mid-sentence. The Art 1 file is mislabeled and actually contains this article, not 'The Russells Did Not Go Chapter'.
